rulings.Case Title: EDWARD BYRD et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING CO., INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment And Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 10, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant DKC Holdings, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and premises owner/contractor liability and off calendar as to strict liability. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Byrd was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant failed to address all persons with knowledge identified by plaintiffs and to show that all such persons did not have knowledge supporting plaintiffs' claims. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, the deposition testimony and declarations of Edward Byrd, Jr. and John Gossett and the declaration of Charles Ay would create a triable issue whether Mr. Byrd was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If the court reaches plaintiffs' evidence, defendant's objections to the declarations of Edward Byrd, Jr. and John Gossett are overruled. Defendant's objections to the George Kirk deposition testimony are sustained. Defendant's objections to the Kaiser Gypsum Asbestos Information Act document are sustained. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are sustained as to his opinions about the joint compound and overruled as to his opinions about insulation. Defendant's motion is also denied as to its arguments related to the peculiar risk doctrine and standard of care. Plaintiffs represent they have dismissed their causes of action for strict liability. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD BYRD et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING CO., INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 10, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant L.E. Wentz Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Byrd was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Edward Byrd, Jr.'s declaration are sustained as to his statements regarding identifying Wentz employees based on hard hats and t-shirts and his use of the phrase "asbestos-containing" in paragraph 6 and overruled as to the remainder of his declaration. Defendant's objections to the deposition testimony of George Kirk are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD BYRD et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING CO., INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 10, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant The W.W. Henry Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Byrd was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT I LEWIS VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 10, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Tutor-Saliba Corporation's demurrer to the third cause of action alleged in plaintiff's complaint is overruled. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff's complaint fails for uncertainty or fails to state a cause of action against defendant. The Court will not take judicial notice of the Articles of Incorporation attached by defendant. The Court will not take judicial notice of the website printout attached by plaintiff. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
